summary: Deleted the remaining assembly source file `asm/melee/ft/ftdemo.s`, removing 421 lines of hand/disassembled PowerPC for fighter demo routines and associated local data. The PR contains no discussion or review comments, so the exact upstream reason is not stated, but in decompilation workflow this kind of PR typically indicates that the assembly fallback was no longer needed because equivalent C/object coverage existed elsewhere.

key_files.details: Removed 421 lines containing `ftDemo_CreateFighter`, `ftDemo_ObjAllocInit`, `ftDemo_SetArchiveData`, `ftDemo_GetMotionFileString`, `ftDemo_SetFacingDirection`, local callback/data tables, string literals, and small data constants.

what_changed: Deleted assembly definitions for global ftdemo routines including `ftDemo_CreateFighter`, `ftDemo_ObjAllocInit`, `ftDemo_SetArchiveData`, `ftDemo_GetMotionFileString`, and `ftDemo_SetFacingDirection`.
Deleted local data from the assembly file, including `on_create_fighter`, `ints$243`, string literal objects such as `@256` and `@257`, and small-data constants such as `@258` and `@261`.
The diff shows no C source or header changes in this PR slice; only the assembly fallback file was removed.
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrowly scoped: one deleted assembly file with no unrelated edits.

Removed obsolete assembly only after the project could presumably build without it; this is the standard safe cleanup step once matching C has taken over, though the slice does not show the replacement source.
Eliminated duplicated low-level symbol definitions for ftdemo routines, reducing future confusion about whether the source of truth is C or assembly.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled C translation is accepted or otherwise present, remove the corresponding `asm/melee/.../*.s` fallback in a separate focused cleanup when possible.
Assembly-removal PRs are easiest to review and search when the title names the exact `.s` file being removed.
If removing an asm file that contained local data tables and literals, verify the replacement object still accounts for both `.text` and data/sdata/sdata2 contributions, not only the functions.
This slice provides evidence only for removal, not for the matching technique used to replace the assembly.
naming_conventions: `ftDemo_*` was the symbol prefix used by the removed demo fighter routines.
The deleted assembly referenced broader fighter subsystem naming patterns such as `Fighter_*`, `ftData_*`, `ftParts_*`, `ftAnim_*`, `ftCo_*`, `ftColl_*`, and `ftLib_*`.

The assembly file used local autogenerated labels and literal names such as `.L_00000130`, `ints$243`, `@256`, `@257`, `@258`, and `@261`, which replacement C should generally express through meaningful static data or compiler-generated equivalents rather than preserving asm-style names unless required for matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The deleted file included `.text`, `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` sections, so a successful replacement needed to match code plus local tables, string literals, padding/gaps, and floating-point constants.

`ftDemo_CreateFighter` in the removed asm showed a long initialization sequence touching GObj creation, GX setup, fighter data allocation, data loading, callbacks, parts/animation/collision setup, camera/shadow setup, and GObj process registration; any C replacement would need to preserve call order and callback table behavior.

The removed assembly included indirect calls through tables such as `ftData_UnkDemoCallbacks0`, `ftData_OnLoad`, and `on_create_fighter`; matching C should model those as function-pointer tables or existing data arrays with correct indexing.

The removed `ftDemo_GetMotionFileString` path included an `OSReport` plus `__assert` fallback when a callback was missing, indicating that diagnostic/assert strings and line numbers can be part of matching-sensitive data.
The evidence does not include the replacement C diff, so no specific matching trick, register allocation fix, or struct-layout adjustment can be confirmed from this PR alone.
follow_up_queries: Find the PR or commit that added/matched the C implementation replacing `asm/melee/ft/ftdemo.s`.
Inspect current `src/melee/ft/ftdemo.c` and related headers for the final names and struct types corresponding to the deleted assembly routines.
Search for later renames of `ftDemo_CreateFighter`, `ftDemo_SetArchiveData`, and `ftDemo_GetMotionFileString` to understand finalized naming conventions.
